justification: All pass criteria met: SLA math clearly identified with exact numbers (99.95% × 99.9% = 99.85%), multiple mitigation options recommended (async KYC with pending state, circuit breaker + fast-fail, formal SLA reduction to 99.85%), circuit breaker gap explicitly called out as critical, health endpoint / Onfido coupling identified with cascading failure explanation, verdict is NOT READY with escalation to architect and product for SLA decision.

observations.text: SLA math is presented as the headline finding with precise arithmetic (99.85% composite), framed as certainty not risk
Circuit breaker gap identified with clear explanation of thread pool exhaustion cascade
Health check coupling to Onfido identified with specific cascading failure scenario (LB pulling healthy pods)
Three distinct mitigation paths offered: async KYC with pending state, circuit breaker pattern, or formally lower SLA
Excellent depth beyond rubric requirements: idempotency gaps, timeout headroom analysis, failure mode table, cost implications of duplicate checks
Actionable escalation table routing issues to correct stakeholders
Distinguishes liveness vs readiness probes as the fix for health check coupling
